name: First United Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured West Texas bank offering personal and business banking, mortgages, treasury management, and specialized financial services since 1907.
description_long:
First United Bank traces its roots to 1907 as The First State Bank of Dimmitt and has evolved into a full-service regional bank serving West Texas communities. The bank operates under the "Spirit of West Texas" philosophy, built on four core principles: Generosity, Loyalty, Integrity, and Community. With over 100 years of banking history in the region, the institution positions itself as deeply rooted in local markets and committed to understanding the distinct financial needs of West Texas residents and businesses.

The bank offers comprehensive personal and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, credit products, and specialized services. Personal banking includes mortgages through their Spirit Mortgage division with online pre-qualification tools and loan officers. Business customers access commercial checking, savings, loans, lines of credit, and treasury management services including eDeposit Merchant Capture, Positive Pay, merchant card services, and cash management solutions. Additional offerings include Freedom First Prepaid & Payroll Cards, HSA and IRA service centers, and ATM/Visa credit card access.

First United Bank differentiates itself through regional focus and institutional longevity. Rather than operating as a national chain, the bank emphasizes its West Texas heritage and deep community involvement, including a branded podcast ("The Spirit of West Texas") featuring local business leaders and community figures. The treasury management services suggest sophisticated business banking capabilities, while the mortgage division indicates retail lending competency. FDIC insurance and federal backing provide standard deposit protections.

As a regional bank, First United Bank serves customers seeking personalized service with institutional knowledge of West Texas markets. However, the website provides limited detail on interest rates, fees, account minimums, competitive positioning, or specific product features. The regional focus may limit convenience for customers outside West Texas service areas. Digital banking capabilities appear available but are not extensively detailed on the website.
services: Business loans and lines of credit
eDeposit Merchant Capture (check scanning and remote deposit)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Over 100 years of continuous banking history in West Texas since 1907
Comprehensive treasury management services including eDeposit Merchant Capture for businesses
Spirit Mortgage division with online pre-qualification tools and streamlined loan process
Business banking expertise with loans, lines of credit, and cash management services
Multiple service channels including mobile app banking access
Community-focused institution with documented commitment to local development
cons: Regional bank with service area limited to West Texas, reducing convenience for customers elsewhere
Website lacks specific information on interest rates, APYs, fees, or account minimums
Limited transparency on mortgage rates, terms, or qualification requirements beyond basic pre-qualification tool
Minimal details on digital banking features, online account management capabilities, or security protocols
best_for: West Texas small business owners seeking personalized treasury management and commercial lending
Regional homebuyers looking for local mortgage expertise and streamlined loan processes
Small-to-medium businesses needing merchant services and cash management solutions
West Texas residents seeking community-focused banking with local decision-making
diagnosis: First United Bank is best suited for West Texas businesses and residents who value personalized regional banking and community connection over national brand convenience. The main caveat is that the bank's regional focus limits accessibility for customers outside West Texas, and the website does not provide sufficient detail on competitive rates, fees, or specific product terms needed for thorough comparison shopping.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
